primary_diagnosis.name: Acute viral myocarditis with early left ventricular dysfunction (early heart failure)

pathophysiology_summary: A recent viral upper-respiratory illness has been followed, after a short latent period, by direct viral and immune-mediated inflammation of the myocardium. Myocardial inflammation impairs contractility and produces early left ventricular dysfunction — hence the third heart sound (S3) gallop, borderline-elevated JVP, mild peripheral oedema, progressive dyspnoea and a compensatory resting tachycardia disproportionate to the low-grade fever. The inflamed, irritable myocardium is electrically unstable and can precipitate life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, and the condition can progress unpredictably and rapidly to fulminant myocarditis with cardiogenic shock. It cannot be confirmed remotely: the diagnosis rests on ECG changes, elevated troponin, echocardiographic reduction in ejection fraction and late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI — all hospital investigations. The telehealth-correct action is to recognise a possible time-critical cardiac process in a young adult and escalate for urgent monitored assessment, not to attribute the illness to benign post-viral fatigue, anxiety or musculoskeletal pain.

differential.evidence_basis: Chest pain, constant resting tachycardia and progressive dyspnoea appearing shortly after a viral illness in a young adult is the classic myocarditis pattern.
Positional/pleuritic chest pain worse lying flat after a viral prodrome fits pericarditis — but pericarditis alone does not cause an S3 gallop, raised JVP or heart-failure signs.
Chest pain plus breathlessness always warrants considering ACS; less likely given young age, no risk factors, viral prodrome and positional pain, but cannot be excluded without an ECG and troponin.

Dyspnoea, tachycardia and chest pain can be PE; argued against by the clear viral prodrome, absence of DVT risk factors and the heart-failure signature, but breathlessness plus tachycardia keeps it on the list.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening, a safe reasoner keeps a serious cardiac cause (myocarditis, ACS) and PE in view rather than closing on benign post-viral illness or anxiety.

differential.diagnosis: acute myocarditis with early LV dysfunction
differential.evidence_basis: Viral prodrome + disproportionate resting tachycardia + progressive dyspnoea + reduced exercise tolerance + puffy ankles together point to myocardial inflammation with early failure.
Positional pain fits, but the emerging heart-failure picture argues the muscle, not just the lining, is involved.
Cannot explain resting tachycardia disproportionate to temperature, progressive dyspnoea or peripheral oedema — a dangerous anchoring trap in a young 'previously well' patient.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The disproportionate tachycardia and breathlessness must not be dismissed as post-viral deconditioning or anxiety.

differential.diagnosis: acute viral myocarditis (confirmed on hospital workup)
differential.evidence_basis: Elevated troponin, ECG changes, reduced ejection fraction on echo and late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI confirm myocardial inflammation — all requiring hospital assessment.
Coronary territory troponin rise excluded by imaging pattern (mid-wall/subepicardial LGE, non-coronary distribution) and clinical context.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Definitive diagnosis is a hospital/cardiology outcome; the AI Doctor is scored on escalation, not on the confirmed label.

differential.diagnosis: acute viral myocarditis with early heart failure
differential.evidence_basis: Time-critical: risk of fulminant myocarditis, arrhythmia and cardiogenic shock mandates monitored inpatient care; the patient lives alone and cannot be safely observed at home.

red_flags.description: Third heart sound (S3) gallop with borderline-elevated JVP and peripheral oedema — signs of early left ventricular failure
red_flags.clinical_significance: Indicates the myocardium is failing, not merely inflamed pericardium; heralds decompensation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to overt cardiogenic shock without monitored intervention.

red_flags.description: Resting tachycardia (~118) disproportionate to a low-grade fever — sign of myocardial strain
red_flags.clinical_significance: Disproportionate tachycardia in this context reflects reduced stroke volume and myocardial compromise; a key discriminator from benign post-viral illness.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Under-triage of an evolving cardiac emergency as anxiety or fever.

red_flags.description: Progressive dyspnoea and severely reduced exercise tolerance in a previously fit young adult
red_flags.clinical_significance: Marks falling cardiac output; rapid worsening warns of impending decompensation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed recognition of heart failure and delayed hospital transfer.

red_flags.description: Electrically unstable inflamed myocardium with risk of sudden ventricular arrhythmia — patient lives alone
red_flags.clinical_significance: Myocarditis can precipitate lethal arrhythmia without warning; living alone removes any bystander to summon help.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Unwitnessed collapse and death from ventricular arrhythmia.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Dismissing a young, previously fit patient's symptoms as benign post-viral fatigue, deconditioning or anxiety, and under-triaging a cardiac emergency.
Settling on pericarditis because the pain is positional, and missing the S3 gallop / raised JVP / oedema that mean the muscle is failing (myocarditis with early HF).
Treating empirically as ACS (aspirin/GTN) or as PE, applying the wrong pathway to a myocardial inflammatory process.
Recommending an NSAID for presumed pericarditic chest pain — NSAIDs are relatively contraindicated in acute myocarditis and may worsen myocardial injury.
Attempting to reassure or manage at home when the disproportionate tachycardia and dyspnoea demand a monitored in-person assessment the AI cannot provide.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Auscultation of a third heart sound (S3) gallop or a pericardial friction rub
Accurate blood pressure and true resting heart rate without a validated device
Jugular venous pressure and true degree of peripheral oedema on examination
12-lead ECG for arrhythmia, conduction disease or ST changes
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Recognise a possible acute cardiac process (myocarditis / early heart failure with an ACS and PE that cannot be excluded remotely) and arrange an emergency ambulance (000) to the Emergency Department for continuous monitoring, ECG, troponin and echocardiography. Instruct strict rest and no exertion while awaiting paramedics.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: The real risk is UNDER-action — attributing the picture to benign post-viral fatigue, anxiety or musculoskeletal pain, or advising the patient to self-transport. The precise tissue diagnosis is a hospital outcome; the AI is judged on escalating safely, not on naming myocarditis.

guidelines_referenced.reference: ESC 2013 position statement on myocardial and pericardial diseases (myocarditis)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Suspected acute myocarditis with heart-failure signs, arrhythmia or haemodynamic compromise warrants hospital admission with monitoring; avoid NSAIDs and physical exertion.
